title: RNA extraction for Kryptoperidinium triquetrum and Nitzschia laevis
author: Will Lewis

steps.title: Take 50 µl aliquot into a sterile 1
steps.instruction: Take 50 µl aliquot into a sterile 1.5 mL tube that is suitable for use with a pestle grinder. Store remaining 250 µL sample at -20 °C to be processed as needed in the future.

steps.title: Pestle grind 50 µl sample for...
steps.instruction: Pestle grind 50 µl sample for 1 minute to lyse the cells.

steps.title: Add 750 µl TRIzol to the...
steps.instruction: Add 750 µl TRIzol to the lysed sample and mix by pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Pellet cell fragments by centrifugation at...
steps.instruction: Pellet cell fragments by centrifugation at 12,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4 °C and transfer the supernatant to a new sterile tube.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.25 volumes of chloroform per volume of sample (measure sample volume using a pipette) and thoroughly mix by vortexing for 15 seconds.

steps.title: Store sample at -20°C
steps.instruction: Store sample at -20°C

steps.title: Analyse samples using Nanodrop to ensure...
steps.instruction: Analyse samples using Nanodrop to ensure the 260/280 ratio is ~2.0 and the 260/230 ratio is between 2.0 and 2.2.

steps.title: Measure concentration of RNA and DNA...
steps.instruction: Measure concentration of RNA and DNA using Qubit BR assay kits.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes

steps.title: Remove the upper RNA-containing aqueous phase...
steps.instruction: Remove the upper RNA-containing aqueous phase of the sample after phase separation and transfer this to a new sterile tube. Make sure not to pipette too close to the interface to avoid contaminating the sample with DNA. Only very small quantities of RNA are needed for RNA-seq, so no need to try and collect the total aqueous phase, as this will decrease sample quality.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 15 minutes at 4 °C for phase separation

steps.title: Transfer the ethanol and aqueous phase...
steps.instruction: Transfer the ethanol and aqueous phase mixture into a Zymo-Spin IICR Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge at 15 000 x g for 30 seconds. Transfer the column into a new collection tube and discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Gently add 0
steps.instruction: Gently add 0.6 volumes of ethanol to aqueous phase and mix by gentle inversion

steps.title: To elute RNA, add 40 μl...
steps.instruction: To elute RNA, add 40 μl of DNase/RNase-Free Water directly to the column matrix and centrifuge.

steps.title: Add 700 μl RNA Wash Buffer5...
steps.instruction: Add 700 μl RNA Wash Buffer5 to the column and centrifuge at 15 000 x g for for 1 minute to ensure complete removal of the wash buffer. Transfer the column carefully into an RNase-free tube.

steps.title: Add 400 μl Direct-zol RNA PreWash...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μl Direct-zol RNA PreWash to the column and centrifuge at 15 000 x g for 30 seconds. Discard the flow-through and repeat this step.

steps.title: DNase I treatment
steps.instruction: DNase I treatment

steps.title: Add 400 μl RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μl RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge at 15 000 x g for 30 seconds.

steps.title: In an RNase-free tube (not included),...
steps.instruction: In an RNase-free tube (not included), add 5 μl DNase I (6 U/μl)*, 75 μl DNA Digestion Buffer and mix by gentle inversion. Add the mix directly to the column matrix.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (20-30°C) for 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (20-30°C) for 15 minutes. Proceed to next step.

steps.title: The protocol steps in this section...
steps.instruction: The protocol steps in this section have been directly adapted from the manufacturer's protocol for the Direct-zol RNA Miniprep kit: https://files.zymoresearch.com/protocols/_r2050_r2051_r2052_r2053_direct-zol_rna_miniprep.pdf
